question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here is the full structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` for a **Kubernetes (sidecar) charm**, based on the official Charmcraft documentation:

---

## Complete `charmcraft.yaml` for a Kubernetes Charm

```yaml
# ── Identity ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
type: charm                        # REQUIRED – must be "charm"
name: my-k8s-app                   # REQUIRED – charm identifier on Charmhub
title: My Kubernetes App           # Recommended – human-readable name
summary: A one-line description.   # REQUIRED – max 78 characters
description: |                     # REQUIRED – detailed multi-line description
  This charm deploys and manages My Kubernetes App on a Juju-managed
  Kubernetes cluster using the sidecar pattern.

# ── Runtime Base ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
base: ubuntu@24.04                 # REQUIRED – OS the charm runs on
build-base: ubuntu@24.04           # Recommended – OS used during build

# ── Feature Requirements ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
assumes:
  - k8s-api                        # Declares this charm requires a K8s model
  - juju >= 3.3

# ── Kubernetes Containers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# REQUIRED for K8s sidecar charms. Maps container names to their OCI images.
containers:
  app:
    resource: app-image            # References an entry under `resources`
    mounts:
      - storage: data              # References an entry under `storage`
        location: /var/lib/app
  # Optional: a sidecar container built from a base (no resource key)
  log-agent:
    bases:
      - name: ubuntu
        channel: "24.04"
        architectures: [amd64, arm64]
    mounts:
      - storage: logs
        location: /var/log/app

# ── Resources ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
resources:
  app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for the main application container

# ── Storage ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    description: Persistent data directory
    location: /var/lib/app
    minimum-size: 1G
  logs:
    type: filesystem
    description: Log output directory
    location: /var/log/app

# ── Configuration Options ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
config:
  options:
    port:
      type: int
      default: 8080
      description: Port the application listens on
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: INFO
      description: "Logging level: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR"
    tls-enabled:
      type: boolean
      default: false
      description: Enable TLS termination

# ── Actions ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
actions:
  rotate-credentials:
    description: Rotate application credentials
    params:
      dry-run:
        type: boolean
        default: false
        description: Simulate the rotation without applying changes
    required: []

# ── Integration Endpoints ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
provides:
  http:
    interface: http
    description: Exposes an HTTP endpoint for ingress or other consumers

requires:
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    limit: 1
  database:
    interface: postgresql_client
    limit: 1
    optional: true

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: my-k8s-app-peers

# ── Charm Libraries ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
charm-libs:
  - lib: traefik_k8s.v2.ingress
    version: "2"
  - lib: data_platform_libs.v0.database_requires
    version: "0.20"

# ── Build Parts ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
parts:
  charm:
    plugin: charm
    charm-entrypoint: src/charm.py
    charm-requirements:
      - requirements.txt

# ── Links ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
links:
  documentation: https://discourse.charmhub.io/t/my-k8s-app
  issues:
    - https://github.com/my-org/my-k8s-app-operator/issues
  source:
    - https://github.com/my-org/my-k8s-app-operator
  website:
    - https://my-app.example.com

# ── Optional: Execution User ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Requires Juju >= 3.6.0
charm-user: non-root               # Options: root | sudoer | non-root
```

---

## Key Rules for Kubernetes Charms

| Rule | Detail |
|---|---|
| **`containers` is required** | Every K8s sidecar charm must declare at least one container |
| **`k8s-api` in `assumes`** | Prevents accidental deployment on machine models |
| **`resources` must match `containers`** | If a container uses `resource:`, a matching `oci-image` entry in `resources` is required |
| **`storage` is referenced by containers** | Container `mounts` must reference a storage name defined under `storage` |
| **No `bases` key** | The old `bases` top-level key is deprecated; use `base` + `build-base` + `platforms` instead |
| **Don't edit generated files** | Charmcraft auto-generates `metadata.yaml`, `config.yaml`, and `actions.yaml` from this file — edit only `charmcraft.yaml` |

---

## Minimal Valid Example

If you just need the smallest possible working `charmcraft.yaml`:

```yaml
type: charm
name: my-k8s-app
summary: My minimal K8s charm.
description: Deploys my app on Kubernetes.
base: ubuntu@24.04

assumes:
  - k8s-api

containers:
  app:
    resource: app-image

resources:
  app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for the app

parts:
  charm:
    plugin: charm
```

This is the smallest set of fields Charmcraft will accept for a Kubernetes charm. Everything else — relations, config, actions, storage — is added as your charm grows.
